title: Magnetic Position and Orientation Tracking System
authors: Raab, Frederick H.; Blood, Ernest B.; Steiner, Terry O.; Jones, Herbert R.
venue: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
keywords: 6-DOF; magnetic dipole; AC tracking; foundational
notes: Seminal formalization of quasi-static magnetic-dipole 6-DOF tracking. DOI inferred from IEEE TAES AES-15(5); page range 709-718 confirmed via multiple indexes.

title: Electromagnetic Tracking in Medicine—A Review of Technology, Validation, and Applications
authors: Franz, Alfred M.; Haidegger, Tamás; Birkfellner, Wolfgang; Cleary, Kevin; Peters, Terry M.; Maier-Hein, Lena
keywords: review; medical; validation; applications
notes: The definitive modern review of medical EMT. DOI and citation verified via PubMed/dblp.

title: Design and application of an assessment protocol for electromagnetic tracking systems
authors: Hummel, Johann B.; Bax, Michael R.; Figl, Michael L.; Kang, Yan; Maurer, Calvin R.; Birkfellner, Wolfgang W.; Bergmann, Helmar; Shahidi, Ramin
keywords: assessment protocol; accuracy; phantom; Aurora; microBIRD
notes: Standardized phantom/protocol for comparable EMT accuracy assessment. DOI verified. Volume/issue/pages to re-confirm against Wiley record.

title: A Novel Method for Nonfluoroscopic Catheter-Based Electroanatomical Mapping of the Heart: In Vitro and In Vivo Accuracy Results
authors: Gepstein, Lior; Hayam, Gal; Ben-Haim, Shlomo A.
keywords: CARTO; electroanatomical mapping; electrophysiology; clinical
notes: Foundational clinical paper underlying the Biosense CARTO system. PMID 9118532 verified.

title: Systematic distortions in magnetic position digitizers
authors: Birkfellner, Wolfgang; Watzinger, Franz; Wanschitz, Felix; Ewers, Rolf; Bergmann, Helmar
keywords: distortion; metal; eddy current; ferromagnetic; characterization
notes: Early systematic characterization of field distortion in magnetic digitizers. DOI/pages to re-confirm against Wiley record; citation widely reported as Med Phys 25(11):2242-2248.

title: A survey of electromagnetic position tracker calibration techniques
keywords: calibration; distortion correction; polynomial fit; interpolation
notes: Survey of EMT calibration/distortion-correction methods (interpolation, polynomial, shape functions). DOI verified; volume/issue/pages to re-confirm.

title: A new method for magnetic position and orientation tracking
authors: Paperno, Eugene; Sasada, Ichiro; Leonovich, Eduard
keywords: rotating field; 2-axis excitation; 3-axis sensing; phase/amplitude
notes: Rotating quasi-static field method; alternative excitation/decoding strategy. DOI to re-confirm against IEEE record.

title: 3-D Magnetic Tracking of a Single Subminiature Coil with a Large 2-D Array of Uniaxial Transmitters
keywords: transmitter array; subminiature coil; subarray; computation
notes: Array-of-transmitters architecture with subarray tracking for speed. DOI/article-id to re-confirm against IEEE record.

title: Magnetic Sensors and Their Applications
authors: Lenz, James E.; Edelstein, Alan S.
keywords: magnetic sensors; search coil; fluxgate; Hall; AMR; GMR; MTJ; review
notes: Canonical review comparing all major magnetic sensor families incl. search coil, fluxgate, Hall, AMR, GMR, magnetic tunnel junctions. Vol 6, pp 631-649 confirmed; DOI to re-confirm against IEEE record.

title: Magnetoresistive sensor detectivity: A comparative analysis
authors: Davies, J. E.; Watts, J. D.; Novotny, J.; Huang, D.; Eames, P. G.
keywords: detectivity; 1/f noise; GMR; TMR; MTJ; die area; fundamental limit
notes: Comparative noise/detectivity analysis of GMR vs TMR(MTJ); finds detectivity scales with die area and converges across types when normalized for linear range. DOI verified via AIP.

title: Electromagnetic tracking in the clinical environment
authors: Yaniv, Ziv; Wilson, Emmanuel; Lindisch, David; Cleary, Kevin
keywords: clinical EMT; accuracy; distortion; interventional; assessment
notes: Clinical-environment characterization of EMT accuracy and distortion. Citation 36(3):876-892, DOI 10.1118/1.3075829, PMID 19378748 verified.

title: Aurora Electromagnetic Tracking System — Sensors (5DOF/6DOF, micro 0.3 mm)
keywords: Aurora; 5DOF; 6DOF; micro sensor; catheter; vendor
notes: Vendor source for medical-grade sensor form factors (e.g., micro 5DOF ~0.3 mm dia). Vendor-reported; access year approximate.

title: IEC 60601-1 Medical electrical equipment — Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance
keywords: medical safety; leakage current; means of protection; isolation; Type BF/CF
notes: Governing safety standard for medical electrical equipment. Patient leakage limits e.g. Type CF 10 uA normal / 50 uA single-fault; two Means of Protection required. Edition history per IEC webstore.

title: IEC 60601-1-2 Medical electrical equipment — Part 1-2: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance — Collateral standard: Electromagnetic disturbances — Requirements and tests
keywords: EMC; electromagnetic compatibility; risk-based; essential performance; immunity
notes: EMC collateral standard. 4th ed (2014) shifted to risk/intended-use-based tests by environment (professional healthcare / home / special). Edition 4.1 = Amendment 1 (2020).

title: The Art of Electronics, 3rd Edition
keywords: analog; low-noise amplifier; op-amp noise; instrumentation amplifier; reference
notes: Standard reference for low-noise analog design (amplifier voltage/current noise, noise matching, instrumentation amps). ISBN 978-0521809269.

title: Analog-to-Digital Converter Survey and Analysis
venue: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
keywords: ADC; survey; resolution vs sampling rate; jitter; thermal limit; figure of merit
notes: Canonical ADC survey; establishes resolution-vs-rate trends and limiting mechanisms (thermal at low rate, ~1 bit/octave fall-off at high rate; aperture jitter). DOI 10.1109/49.761034 verified.

title: IEEE Standard for Terminology and Test Methods for Analog-to-Digital Converters
keywords: ADC test; ENOB; SINAD; SNR; FFT test; terminology
notes: Defines ADC error terminology and FFT-based test methods (ENOB from SINAD, etc.). Multiple editions (2000/2010/2023); cite the edition used in a given measurement.

title: Frequency-domain description of a lock-in amplifier
keywords: lock-in amplifier; synchronous detection; phase-sensitive; noise bandwidth
notes: Clear pedagogical/quantitative treatment of lock-in (synchronous) detection. DOI 10.1119/1.17629 verified.

title: A New Approach to Linear Filtering and Prediction Problems
venue: Transactions of the ASME — Journal of Basic Engineering
keywords: Kalman filter; state estimation; recursive; prediction; foundational
notes: The foundational Kalman filter paper. Vol 82(1):35-45, DOI 10.1115/1.3662552 verified.

title: Unscented Filtering and Nonlinear Estimation
authors: Julier, Simon J.; Uhlmann, Jeffrey K.
keywords: unscented Kalman filter; UKF; unscented transform; nonlinear estimation
notes: Definitive review of the unscented transform/UKF for nonlinear estimation. DOI 10.1109/JPROC.2003.823141 verified.

title: Estimation with Applications to Tracking and Navigation: Theory, Algorithms and Software
authors: Bar-Shalom, Yaakov; Li, X. Rong; Kirubarajan, Thiagalingam
keywords: estimation; tracking; Kalman; EKF; data association; CRLB
notes: Standard reference for estimation/tracking (KF/EKF, filtering, CRLB, fusion). ISBN 978-0471416555; DOI 10.1002/0471221279.

title: An Algorithm for Least-Squares Estimation of Nonlinear Parameters
venue: Journal of the Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics
keywords: Levenberg-Marquardt; nonlinear least squares; optimization; damping
notes: The Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm; workhorse nonlinear solver for EMT pose. DOI 10.1137/0111030 verified.

title: Fundamentals of Statistical Signal Processing, Volume I: Estimation Theory
keywords: MLE; Cramer-Rao bound; CRLB; Bayesian estimation; Fisher information
notes: Standard reference for MLE, CRLB/Fisher information, and Bayesian estimation used in Ch. 23-24. ISBN 0133457117.

title: Numerical Optimization, 2nd Edition
venue: Springer (Springer Series in Operations Research and Financial Engineering)
keywords: Gauss-Newton; trust region; line search; conditioning; convergence
notes: Standard numerical-optimization reference (Gauss-Newton, trust-region/LM, convergence, conditioning). ISBN 9780387303031.

title: Interference during the use of an electromagnetic tracking system under OR conditions
keywords: interference; OR conditions; ambient noise; ferromagnetic; EM susceptibility
notes: Quantifies EMT interference under operating-room conditions: ambient EM noise error <0.15 mm RMS, but ferromagnetic/electrical devices up to 8.4 mm / 166 deg. DOI verified; PMID 12020992.

title: Constant TMR magnetic field sensor detectivity with bias voltage
authors: Monteblanco, E.; Solignac, A.; Chopin, C.; Moulin, J.; Belliot, P.; Belin, N.; Campiglio, P.; Fermon, C.; Pannetier-Lecoeur, M.
venue: arXiv preprint (cf. published version in Sensors)
keywords: TMR; bias voltage; detectivity; 1/f noise; Hooge; magnetic tunnel junction
notes: Shows TMR detectivity stays roughly constant vs bias voltage because sensitivity and noise scale together — central to the 'bias-reference noise in biased sensors' error term. arXiv:2103.04750 (authors verified); a published Sensors version exists (to link).

title: Enhancing electromagnetic tracking accuracy in medical applications using pre-trained witness sensor distortion models
venue: International Journal of Computer Assisted Radiology and Surgery
keywords: witness sensor; distortion compensation; pre-trained model; C-arm; ultrasound probe; online
notes: Witness-sensor distortion compensation: measures field variation at a witness position to identify/compensate a distorter without sensors on it; C-arm error reduced to 1.52 mm RMS. DOI verified.

title: Neural network-based calibration of electromagnetic tracking systems
authors: Kindratenko, Volodymyr V.; Sherman, William R.
keywords: neural network; machine learning; calibration; distortion correction; position and orientation
notes: Neural-network calibration compensating position and orientation error; compared to traditional methods. Vol 9(1):70-78, DOI verified.

title: Polhemus History — Over 50 Years of Experience
keywords: Polhemus; history; AC tracking; FASTRAK; LIBERTY; military aviation
notes: Vendor history: founded 1969 by Bill Polhemus, Colchester VT; AC EM tracking originating in military aviation head-tracking. Products FASTRAK/LIBERTY/PATRIOT/G4. Vendor-reported.

title: Our History — Northern Digital Inc. (NDI)
keywords: NDI; Aurora; Polaris; Waterloo; history
notes: Vendor history of NDI (Waterloo, Ontario): optical (Polaris) and EM (Aurora) navigation. Vendor-reported; corroborate dates with independent sources.

title: Ascension Technology of Milton acquired by Roper Industries
keywords: Ascension; Roper; NDI; acquisition; pulsed-DC; Flock of Birds
notes: News report: Ascension (co-founded 1986 by Jack Scully & Ernie Blood; DC 'Flock of Birds') assets sold to Roper Industries (~US$19M, 2012); operated under NDI. Trade-press source.

title: Johnson & Johnson Buys Biosense for $400 Mln in Shares
keywords: Biosense; Ben-Haim; Johnson & Johnson; acquisition; CARTO
notes: News: Biosense (founded 1993 by Shlomo Ben-Haim) acquired by J&J in 1997 (~$400M in shares); became Biosense Webster. Trade-press source; CARTO scientific basis is Gepstein 1997 [gepstein1997].

title: Covidien acquisition of superDimension (electromagnetic navigation bronchoscopy); Medtronic–Covidien close (2015)
keywords: superDimension; ENB; Covidien; Medtronic; bronchoscopy; lung
notes: superDimension ENB acquired by Covidien (~$300M, 2012); Medtronic closed Covidien acquisition Jan 2015. Trade-press/vendor sources; verify exact figures if cited precisely.

title: Electromagnetic Navigation Bronchoscopy for Peripheral Pulmonary Lesions: One-Year Results of the Prospective, Multicenter NAVIGATE Study
authors: Folch, Erik E.; Pritchett, Michael A.; Nead, Matthew A.; et al.
keywords: ENB; navigation bronchoscopy; diagnostic yield; lung lesion; superDimension; clinical outcome
notes: NAVIGATE one-year results: 12-month diagnostic yield ~73%; pneumothorax (grade >=2) 2.9%. PMID 30476574 verified; vol/issue/pages 14(3):445-458 to re-confirm against journal record. 'et al.' — full author list to expand.

title: Optical magnetometry
keywords: optical magnetometry; atomic; SERF; biomagnetic; quantum sensing
notes: Foundational review of optical/atomic magnetometry; SERF sensitivity >1e-15 T/rtHz. Frontier sensor context. DOI 10.1038/nphys566 verified.

title: Sensitivity optimization for NV-diamond magnetometry
authors: Barry, John F.; Schloss, Jennifer M.; Bauch, Erik; Turner, Matthew J.; Hart, Connor A.; Pham, Linh M.; Walsworth, Ronald L.
keywords: NV diamond; nitrogen-vacancy; quantum sensing; magnetometry; sensitivity
notes: Comprehensive review of NV-diamond magnetometry sensitivity limits and improvement strategies. RMP 92(1):015004, DOI verified.

title: Object tracking and orientation determination means, system and process (US 3,868,565)
keywords: Kuipers; SPASYN; nutating field; orthogonal coils; AC/DC; foundational patent
notes: Foundational magnetic position/orientation patent (Polhemus/Kuipers lineage). Uses a nutating field with DC + AC-modulated carrier into orthogonal coils. Cf. companion US 3,983,474 (1976). Resolves the Ch.1 SPASYN open question. Patent number verified via Google Patents/FPO.

title: Device for quantitatively measuring the relative position and orientation of two bodies in the presence of metals utilizing direct current magnetic fields (US 4,945,305)
keywords: Blood; Ascension; pulsed-DC; direct current; metal immunity; 6-DOF
notes: Foundational pulsed/direct-current magnetic tracking patent (Ascension lineage); measures pose in presence of metals using DC fields. Cf. WO1996002008A1 (non-dipole fields). Patent number/inventor/assignee verified.

title: System and method for generating three-dimensional location information of a medical probe (US 5,391,199)
keywords: Ben-Haim; Biosense; CARTO; catheter; sensor coil; three radiator coils
notes: Catheter localization: sensor coil signals from three external radiator coils used to compute location — basis of CARTO (with Acker et al. US 5,558,091 and the broader Biosense patent family). Patent number verified.

title: Anser EMT: the first open-source electromagnetic tracking platform for image-guided interventions
authors: Jaeger, Herman Alexander; Franz, Alfred M.; O'Donoghue, Kilian; Seitel, Alexander; Trauzettel, Fabian; Maier-Hein, Lena; Cantillon-Murphy, Pádraig
venue: International Journal of Computer Assisted Radiology and Surgery
keywords: open-source; FDM; frequency division; lock-in; field generator; 8 coils; OpenIGTLink
notes: Open-source FDM EMT: 8 field-generator coils at distinct frequencies, lock-in demodulation; reported 1.14 mm / 0.04 deg accuracy. Key reference for Ch.19 (FDM), Ch.20 (lock-in), Ch.31 (open-source ecosystem). DOI/PMID verified; vol 12(6):1059-1067 to re-confirm.

title: Adaptive Noise Cancelling: Principles and Applications
authors: Widrow, Bernard; Glover, John R.; McCool, John M.; Kaunitz, John; Williams, Charles S.; Hearn, Robert H.; Zeidler, James R.; Dong, Eugene; Goodlin, Robert C.
keywords: adaptive filter; LMS; noise cancelling; reference sensor; interference
notes: Foundational adaptive noise-cancelling paper (LMS, reference/witness cancellation). Basis for the adaptive-interference and witness-sensor methods of Ch.20/27. DOI 10.1109/PROC.1975.10036 verified.

title: Quaternion kinematics for the error-state Kalman filter
keywords: error-state Kalman filter; quaternion; attitude; Lie group; IMU
notes: Definitive treatment of the error-state (multiplicative) Kalman filter for 3-D orientation — the correct way to estimate rotation without over-parameterization. arXiv:1711.02508 verified.

title: A Tutorial on Particle Filters for Online Nonlinear/Non-Gaussian Bayesian Tracking
authors: Arulampalam, M. Sanjeev; Maskell, Simon; Gordon, Neil; Clapp, Tim
keywords: particle filter; sequential Monte Carlo; resampling; non-Gaussian; importance sampling
notes: Canonical particle-filter tutorial (SIS/SIR, degeneracy, resampling, effective sample size). DOI 10.1109/78.978374 verified.

title: An Economical Class of Digital Filters for Decimation and Interpolation
venue: IEEE Transactions on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
keywords: CIC filter; decimation; multiplier-free; register growth; FPGA
notes: The cascaded integrator-comb (CIC) decimation filter — multiplier-free, the standard front-end decimator for Sigma-Delta ADC streams on FPGA (Ch.18/22). DOI 10.1109/TASSP.1981.1163535 verified.

title: The CORDIC Trigonometric Computing Technique
keywords: CORDIC; rectangular to polar; shift-add; magnitude; phase; FPGA
notes: CORDIC: multiplier-free shift-add computation of magnitude/phase (rectangular->polar), used for the lock-in sqrt(X^2+Y^2)/atan2 on FPGA. DOI verified.

title: Robust Estimation of a Location Parameter
keywords: robust estimation; M-estimator; Huber loss; outliers; influence function
notes: Introduces the Huber M-estimator / robust loss — the basis for outlier-robust pose solving (IRLS) in Ch.23. DOI 10.1214/aoms/1177703732 verified (Project Euclid: vol 35(1):73-101).

title: Principles of GNSS, Inertial, and Multisensor Integrated Navigation Systems, 2nd Edition
keywords: GNSS; dilution of precision; DOP; integrated navigation; Kalman; INS
notes: Standard reference for dilution of precision (DOP) and multisensor (GNSS+INS) integration; cross-domain analogue for EMT conditioning (Ch.24) and fusion (Ch.21). ISBN 978-1-60807-005-3.

title: Evaluation of measurement data — Guide to the expression of uncertainty in measurement (GUM)
authors: Joint Committee for Guides in Metrology (JCGM/BIPM)
keywords: uncertainty; Type A; Type B; combined standard uncertainty; coverage factor; law of propagation
notes: The international metrology standard for uncertainty budgeting: Type A/B evaluation, law of propagation of uncertainty (eq 25.1), combined and expanded uncertainty (k coverage factor). Authoritative basis for the Ch.25 error budget. JCGM 100:2008.

title: Classical Electrodynamics, 3rd Edition
keywords: electrodynamics; magnetic dipole; multipole expansion; near field; quasistatic
notes: Standard graduate electrodynamics reference (dipole fields, multipole expansion, quasistatics, radiation). ISBN 978-0471309321.

title: Introduction to Electrodynamics, 4th Edition
keywords: electrodynamics; magnetostatics; dipole; Faraday; pedagogical
notes: Standard undergraduate electrodynamics reference; clear magnetostatics/dipole/Faraday treatment. ISBN 978-0321856562.

title: The dielectric properties of biological tissues (Parts I–III)
authors: Gabriel, Camelia; Gabriel, Sami; Lau, R. W.; Corthout, E.
keywords: tissue; dielectric; conductivity; permittivity; frequency; Cole-Cole
notes: Definitive measurements/models of tissue conductivity & permittivity (10 Hz-20 GHz). Backs the EMT tissue-transparency claim (low sigma, mu_r~=1 -> skin depth >> body at EMT frequencies). 3-part series; Part II DOI 10.1088/0031-9155/41/11/002, Part III .../003.

title: Inductance Calculations: Working Formulas and Tables
keywords: mutual inductance; self-inductance; coils; formulas; Neumann; coaxial filaments
notes: The classic practical handbook of self/mutual-inductance formulas and tables for coils (coaxial circular filaments, single-layer solenoids, etc.) — design-from-first-principles reference for Ch.5/9/15. Dover reprint ISBN 978-0486495774.

title: The Finite Element Method in Electromagnetics, 3rd Edition
keywords: finite element method; edge elements; Nedelec; eddy current; open boundary; computational EM
notes: Standard computational-electromagnetics reference (vector/edge elements, magnetostatic & eddy-current formulations, open-boundary treatment) for Ch.7. ISBN 978-1118571361.

title: A novel approach to wireless electromagnetic tracking using frequency modulation radio communication
authors: Crowley, Daniel; Cavaliere, Marco; Cantillon-Murphy, Pádraig
venue: International Journal of Computer Assisted Radiology and Surgery
keywords: wireless; FM radio; sensor link; synchronization; Anser; untethered
notes: Wireless EMT: FM-radio transmission of sensor data on the open-source Anser system; 1.61 mm / 0.04 deg vs 1.14 mm wired. Grounds the wireless-sensor / synchronization architecture (Ch.8 §8.6, Ch.10). DOI/PMID verified.

title: Measuring position and orientation using magnetic fields (US 6,073,043)
keywords: dual coil; 6-DOF; two-coil sensor; exact field coupling; catheter; orientation
notes: Dual-coil 6-DOF magnetic sensor using exact (non-approximate) field coupling; basis for two-askew-coil catheter sensors (Ch.13 §13.3). Larger inter-axis angle gives better orientation accuracy. Patent number/inventor verified.

title: Our Technology — AC Electromagnetic Tracking
keywords: AC tracking; 6-DOF; vendor
notes: Vendor self-description of AC electromagnetic tracking; used only for vendor-attributed claims. Access year approximate; treat as living document.
